instrument_name: Extract a short recognizable name for the instrument. Use the primary issuer or brand name only, ignoring fund structure, share class, and trailing identifiers. Examples: 'UNITS VANGUARD FDS PLC FTSE ALL WORLD UCITS ETF/USD/DIST' → 'VANGUARD' | 'UNITS AMUNDI IS MSCI WORLD UCITS ETF EUR/ACC' → 'AMUNDI MSCI WORLD' | 'UNITS PIMCO FIXED INC GBL BOND UCITS ETF/GBP/HDGD' → 'PIMCO GLOBAL BOND'.
valuation: Extract and format the numeric value in European standard format for bank documents. Requirements: 1) No currency symbols, 2) Use period (.) as thousands separator for every three digits, 3) Use comma (,) as decimal separator, 4) Always include two decimal places. Examples: 45230.00 becomes 45.230,00 | 1875400.00 becomes 1.875.400,00 | 9876.50 becomes 9.876,50
profit_loss_pct: Extract the percentage value. Requirements: 1) No % symbol, 2) Use comma (,) as decimal separator, 3) Preserve the negative sign if loss. Examples: -8.45% becomes -8,45 | -22.10% becomes -22,10 | 4.33% becomes 4,33.
risk_category: Classify the position based on profit_loss_pct value. Rules: 1) If profit_loss_pct is between 0 and -5 (exclusive), assign 'LOW'. 2) If profit_loss_pct is between -5 (inclusive) and -15 (exclusive), assign 'MEDIUM'. 3) If profit_loss_pct is -15 or below, assign 'HIGH'. 4) If profit_loss_pct > 0 (position is in profit), assign 'GAIN'.
